items.briefing:
A DigiCert survey has found that 81% of Australian organisations have already experienced an AI-related security incident or vulnerability — a striking figure that aligns with broader warnings from Australian leaders that AI governance is not keeping pace with how quickly the technology is being adopted. This is particularly relevant for training organisations using AI tools for administration, learning delivery, or communications.

Staff should also be aware that cybercriminals are now using hidden text in emails to trick AI-based security filters, meaning some malicious emails may bypass automated detection. **Practical tip:** If an email feels unusual or unexpected, don't rely on your spam filter alone — report it to your IT contact before clicking any links.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already experiencing AI security incidents, showing immediate national impact.
